selection.status_reason: Live thesis monitor refreshed the active weekly pick.

reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 12.3% and 20-day momentum is 14.2%.
Trend quality is supported by 80% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 12.6% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 2.2%, downside volatility near 1.9%, and a recent max drawdown of 6.9%.
Volume confirmation is -12.1% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 10.9% versus SPY over 5 days and 13.2% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.79, confidence 0.68, and 0.5 weighted articles across 2 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.79, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 3 articles across 2 sources, 0.5 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.67, average source quality 0.75, confidence 0.68.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC -0.02).
Overall model score is 0.223 with high confidence.

news_evidence.title: Lucid Jumps 9%, NIO Rallies 7%, Tesla Rises 4% as EV Stocks Get Charged Up
news_evidence.llm_reason: Sector/peer-driven rally that explicitly notes Tesla up ~4%. Useful for short-term sentiment/momentum but lacks Tesla-specific catalysts (earnings, guidance, production) so impact is modest and likely transitory.

news_evidence.title: Trump Lands In China With $16 Trillion Of CEO Power — And One Big Ask For Xi
news_evidence.llm_reason: Geopolitical/market headline about Trump in China could influence US-China trade sentiment and large-cap/China exposure flows, which indirectly affect Tesla. Article does not mention Tesla or provide company-specific developments, so short-term relevance is low and uncertain.
